old_claim.note: State Medical Supply Stores supports manufacturing, deployment, commercialization, or operational scaling.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains: medieval castle garrison logistics is sourced to castles, garrisons, food, arms, water access, and siege endurance, not to Roman military medical supply stores.

source_shape.source_locator: The Soldiers section describes castle garrisons, constables or castellans, soldiers, weapons, and siege starvation without deriving castle operation from medical supply stores.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: English Heritage describes medieval castle garrison operation through soldiers, command, arms, and siege food pressure.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source identifies the relevant operating system as castle defense and provisioning. Roman hospital supply stores are a different classical medical-logistics node and would overstate an unrelated lineage.

ontology_before: Castle Garrison Logistics depended on Roman Military Medical Supply Stores, implying hospital provisioning scaled medieval castle food, arms, watch, and repair logistics.
ontology_after: Castle Garrison Logistics is sourced to castle defensive operation and provisioning; Roman medical stores remain confined to military medical supply contexts.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: state_medical_supply_stores->castle_garrison_logistics is removed. Preserved: fortification remains the physical substrate for castle garrison operation.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to castle infirmary or medical-supply management rather than full garrison logistics.
A source showed medieval castle provisioning directly derived from Roman military hospital supply stores.
The state_medical_supply_stores node were broadened into all ancient and medieval military stores.
short_reason: Roman medical supply stores are unrelated to castle garrison provisioning.
